full_response:
We start from the definitions

Symmetric (or “symmetrized”) part :
 T_{(ab)} ≡ ½ ( T_{ab} + T_{ba} )        (1)

Antisymmetric part :
 T_{[ab]} ≡ ½ ( T_{ab} − T_{ba} )        (2)

Add (1) and (2):

T_{(ab)} + T_{[ab]}
 = ½ ( T_{ab} + T_{ba} ) + ½ ( T_{ab} − T_{ba} )
 = ½ [ T_{ab} + T_{ba} + T_{ab} − T_{ba} ]
 = ½ [ 2 T_{ab} ]
 = T_{ab}.        (3)

Equation (3) shows that every rank-2 tensor T_{ab} can be decomposed into the sum of its symmetric and antisymmetric parts.

Uniqueness (optional proof):
If T_{ab} = S_{ab} + A_{ab} with S_{ab}=S_{ba} and A_{ab}=−A_{ba}, then
 S_{ab} = ½ ( T_{ab} + T_{ba} ) = T_{(ab)},
 A_{ab} = ½ ( T_{ab} − T_{ba} ) = T_{[ab]},
so the decomposition found above is the only one with the stated symmetry properties.

\boxed{T_{ab}=T_{(ab)}+T_{[ab]}} 

Confidence: 91%
